title: Bigger scout assignment: world continuity preproduction pack
summary: Twentieth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The project now has several approved future directions that all point toward the same bigger feeling: a world that can be walked through, revisited, and noticed as one changing ecological gradient. Corridor travel, phenology, sound, map-return posts, notebook prompts, and field-partner cues had been scoped in isolation. The scout synthesis report is now complete and recommends one clear later grouping: after the authored field-partner cleanup and the first phenology pass, use the first `beach <-> coastal-scrub` corridor proof as the continuity spine, then let broader sound and map-return work follow the proof instead of preceding it.

execution_order.goal: Produce one cohesive world-continuity preproduction pack covering corridor travel, phenology visibility, sound opportunities, and quiet companion/prompt usage across the five-biome chain.
execution_order.why_now: This is the right bigger scout assignment: it stays planning-only, does not block the active main lane, and turns several already-approved future directions into one coherent implementation map.

guardrails: Science accuracy remains a hard gate for shared species, transition ecology, and seasonal or phenology framing.
This is planning-only work; do not implement code.
Do not replace the world map with a fully stitched open world in the recommendation.
Keep corridor advice adjacent-pair oriented and threshold-aware.
Treat phenology as mood-first and authored, not as a heavy calendar simulation.
Treat sound as sparse and supportive, not constant.
Keep notebook-prompt and field-partner opportunities quiet by default and grounded in local readable evidence.
Protect `192x144` readability and the small playfield in every recommendation.
Prefer a few strong signals per transition over giant feature matrices.
acceptance_focus: Scout produces one coherent world-feel plan instead of more isolated sidecars.
Future corridor, phenology, sound, and companion work become easier to sequence and group.
The game's long-term direction stays centered on gradients, change over time, and quiet observation.
Later implementation packets can be chunkier and more confident because synthesis work happened up front.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-world-continuity-preproduction-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-world-continuity-preproduction-pack.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-continuous-corridor-travel-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-hybrid-corridor-travel-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-corridor-edge-content-matrix-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-corridor-threshold-state-audit.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-beach-orientation-fix-options.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-living-world-sequence.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-phenology-grounding-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-phenology-readiness-audit.md, docs/reports/2026-03-28-soundscape-direction-handoff.md, docs/reports/2026-03-29-field-partner-cue-bank-handoff.md, docs/world-travel.md, docs/science-source-ledger.md, and this packet. The synthesis is now done: treat the first `beach <-> coastal-scrub` corridor proof as the preferred post-phenology world-continuity bundle, keep the proof narrow and threshold-readable, and let broader sound plus later map-return work follow that proof instead of shipping in the same first pass.
